title: Why Spaced Repetition Works
content:
Spaced repetition exploits the **spacing effect** — we remember things better when reviews are spread out over time rather than crammed.

The forgetting curve (Ebbinghaus, 1885) shows memory decays exponentially, but each review resets and flattens the curve. Optimal intervals grow geometrically: 1 day → 3 days → 7 days → 21 days → 60 days.

**Key insight:** The goal isn't memorization — it's *integration*. Revisiting ideas at intervals gives your subconscious time to connect them with new experiences and knowledge.

title: Building a Second Brain
content:
Your notes are only as valuable as your ability to **resurface them at the right time**.

Most knowledge management systems fail because they're write-only — you capture ideas but never revisit them. Spaced review solves this by systematically resurfacing your best thinking.

**The workflow:**
1. Capture insights as you encounter them
2. Your AI agent converts key ideas into review cards
3. Cards appear at increasing intervals
4. Rate each card to adjust its frequency
5. Ideas that keep resonating stay in rotation

title: The Pragmatist Wager
content:
When evidence is insufficient to determine which of two beliefs is true, evaluate them by their **pragmatic outcomes**.

Ask: *If I believe A vs B, how does each affect my actions, emotions, and life outcomes?*

This isn't about ignoring truth — it's about what to do when truth is genuinely underdetermined. Choose the belief that leads to better outcomes while remaining open to updating when new evidence arrives.
